(February 26, 2000) (Noah Olivier Smith is born)
(Late 2016) (Lil Yeat begins releasing music online with YNGROY and friends and formed 530)

Leak Date: Before Noah Olivier Smith went by the name "Yeat," he began his musical career under the name "Lil Yeat" in 2017 when he made music under that handle. He and friends, including YNGROY, formed their group "530" from ages 16/17 and released music for fun, nothing serious. Sadly, he has since deleted all of that.

Leak Date: Following his time with 530, Lil Yeat progressed onward, with his own now-deleted EP 3PYEAT. He eventually caught the eye of BigBabyGucci and members of Forever World Records / 1500 and began to collaborate with them. This eventually led to him being signed to BigBabyGucci's label. During this time, his handle on socials was @yeat1500. The cover art used for this era is the avatar for FOREVER WORLD LEAGUE® on SoundCloud.

Notes:
(March 2018) (Yeat and SwagHollywood begin working together)

Leak Date: In an interview with CultureBites from April 18, 2018, Yeat was asked how he met SwagHollywood, and he said his team sent SwagHollywood one of his open verses and he liked it, and he then said that they were "working on a little EP." SwagHollywood tweeted on June 29, 2018, "Me and @yeat1_ dropping a Ep for y’all very soon Swoogang shit," which Yeat retweeted and replied "No cap." However, the collaborative EP never came, with SwagHollywood confirming years later that they never finished the project. The art used for this era is the cover art for "Swoo Life" by SwagHollywood featuring Yeat.

Notes:
(December 26, 2019) (I'm So Me is officially released)
(April 18, 2020) (We Us is officially released)
Leak Date: We Us is the third EP by Portland rapper Yeat. It ties the record for his shortest EP, with three tracks, alongside his debut release Deep Blue Strips. His first project of 2020, the EP would be dropped in April and followed by another EP in September, Hold On. Due to the nature of the title, the project can be seen as falling in between I’m So Me and Up 2 Më in Yeat’s Me/We series.

(Late 2020) (DC2 is scrapped)
Leak Date: DC2, short for Different Creature 2, is the scrapped sequel to Yeat and Clibbo’s collaborative EP Different Creature. Yeat tweeted "DC2" on April 30, 2020. DC2 was most likely cancelled due to many of the songs leaking pre-release. Yeat was also having label issues from 2019 through 2020, so this might also have caused him to not drop, and instead leak the songs himself. This cover was posted to Clibbo's Instagram Story.

Notes:

(September 10, 2020) (Hold Ön is officially released)
Leak Date: Hold Ön is Yeat’s 4th EP after We Us and Different Creature. This EP used to be on all platforms but is now only available on SoundCloud due to unknown reasons. This EP contains 4 songs, but only 2 songs are in the album version on SoundCloud, you can find the other 2 songs if you scroll down far enough in Yeat’s tracks. The 2 songs that are available on the album version on SoundCloud is “Nobu” and “Geeker Day”. This and “We Us” were only released due to issues Yeat was having at the time with his label. So he decided to drop these 2 EP’s in order to keep his fans from waiting a long time for another project.

The project gained traction when the track “Sorry Bout That” took off, accompanied by a music video and TikTok popularity. The mixtape was submitted on June 4, 2021. Notes:
(May 16, 2021) (Yeat begins working on Up 2 Më alongside 4L)
(July 1, 2021) (Yeat begins to tease My Lyfe - EP)
Leak Date: Up 2 Më originally began being worked on by Yeat ahead of the release of 4L, first teased in mid-May of 2021. Several tracks recorded in the sessions prior to 4L's release were in contention for the first iteration of the album, even including tracks such as "WTF," which made the final release of 4L. However, over time, Yeat's plans began to change a little, as he began to shift his focus on working on the now-scrapped My Lyfe - EP and Trëndi - EP, which encapsulate the sessions of the second version of Up 2 Më. The original cover by Yeat was used from May 17, 2021 up until August 21, 2021.

Notes:
(July 1, 2021) (Yeat begins to tease My Lyfe - EP)
(August 5, 2021) (Trëndi is officially released)
Leak Date: Trëndi is Yeat’s third release of 2021, following June’s 4L and April’s Alivë. Released on August 5, 2021, it serves as a teaser for his project, Up 2 Më. Consisting of four tracks, the EP features Yeat’s TwizzyRich labelmate, SeptembersRich, and boasts production from BNYX®, Sharkboy, Rio Leyva, and more. The EP gained attention due to its two singles, “Mad bout that” and “Fukit.” Ahead of its release, Yeat also scrapped an alternate EP that he was working on, titled My Lyfe. These sessions encapsulate the second iteration of the Up 2 Më sessions.

Notes:
(August 5, 2021) (Trëndi is officially released)
(September 10, 2021) (Up 2 Më is officially released)
Leak Date: Up 2 Më is Yeat’s third independently-released album of 2021 and his fourth release of the year, following April’s Alivë, June’s 4L, and August’s Trëndi. He teased the album on Twitter on July 9, 2021. As he explained in an interview with Our Generation Music, the album’s title represents his control over his life and success, “It’s up to me to go up”. Up 2 Më features a multitude of hard-hitting electronic beats layered over Yeat rapping about his extensive drug use, newfound wealth, and his influx of women. Thanks to the viral success of the album’s lead single, “Gët Busy,” Yeat signed a deal with Interscope Records for one million dollars, resulting in this album being Yeat’s final independent release.

Leak Date: 2 Alivë is the sequel to Yeat’s April 2021 project, Alivë, and his first album released through Interscope Records. The album was first teased on December 13, 2021, where Yeat shared the project’s official cover art via social media, teasing fans with a January 2022 release date, however, the album missed the initial deadline. Then on January 31, 2022, Yeat announced in both an Instagram Story and an Instagram post that he had turned the project in. He teased that it would be released between February 11 and February 18. Yeat released the project’s lead single, “Still countin,” along with a music video directed by Cole Bennett on February 11, 2022.

(September 9, 2022) (Lyfë is officially released)
Leak Date: Lyfë is Yeat’s fifth extended play, following his April 2022 deluxe album 2 Alivë (Geëk Pack). First teased by Yeat in an Instagram Story on June 4, 2022, captioned: "Lyfë?" On June 15, 2022, in a now-deleted Tweet, Yeat confirmed this EP would precede the eponymous album, “Fin drop like a 8 song EP den ALBUM [LYFË]…. N change earth n music.”.  In an array of different Instagram Stories posted on August 24, 2022, Yeat would uncover the EP’s twelve-track runtime, as well as the lead single “Talk.” On the night of “Talk”’s release, Yeat would ultimately confirm the EP’s September 9 release date through an Instagram post.

Notes:
(May 4, 2023) (Yeat scraps the deluxe to AftërLyfe and says he would rather drop an album)
(September 17, 2023) (Yeat goes sober and begins to shift his direction and sound)
Leak Date: Lyfëstyle was originally intended to be a 2023 album follow up after AftërLyfe. Yeat announced he would be working on a new album after scrapping a deluxe for his previous album AftërLyfe. On September 8, Yeat announced his new label, Lyfestyle Corporation. He also said "Im back runnin this shit new label new everything andd the album otw itz something crazy." On October 2, he mentions on the label account that he had been sober for 16 days and that the album was coming soon. From this point on, he began to have a drastic change in direction and sound from being sober, transitioning into his "2093" sound. Following the release of 2093, he shifted focus back to LS. The cover art used for this era is the old Lyfestyle Corporation logo from September 13, 2023.

Leak Date: 2093 is Yeat’s 4th studio album. On October 2, 2023 he announced on the label Instagram account that he had been sober for 15 days and that the album was coming soon. From this point on there was a drastic change in his direction, going for a more "futuristic" sound, with the title "2093" taking direct inspiration from the 2009 movie Mr. Nobody, which takes place in 2092. On January 31, 2024, he announced the album would drop in February. On February 14, he confirmed the album for February 16. He continued the rollout in phases, dropping more and more tracks for his fans in multiple phases, with Phase 2 on February 17 and Phase 3 on February 21.

On November 30, 2023, a leaker posted an "official tracklist" for SoFaygo's then-upcoming album Never Apologize, which included a track titled "Watdafck (Feat. Yeat)." However, the legitamacy of this assertion is unknown, and era unconfirmed.

In a Complex cover story released on October 22, 2025, Yeat said the following when asked about Donald Glover (aka Childish Gambino): "We have more records, too. We were in Paris one time making this song... I found a sample and made a beat from scratch while he was singing. I engineered him, too. He's almost like me with melodies. We'll be in the middle of a conversation and he'll start humming a melody and then start singing something. It's inspiring. We made the craziest song. It's got to come out someday."

Name (Join The Discord!):
Childish Gambino - Cruisin' [V3]
(feat. Yeat)
(Cruisin' Thru Africa)
Notes: On January 7, 2024, Yeat posted an Instagram Story captioned "me n gambino going chips for grass and yall would never know," first teasing a Childish Gambino collaboration. On January 31, Yeat announced that his album 2093 was going to drop in February in an Instagram slideshow captioned "2093ISCOMING...... 2.??.42," including an image of him in the studio with Childish Gambino, once again suggesting teasing an appearance on what ended up being on "Power Trip." On February 24, 2024, Yeat posted the credits of "Power Trip" with Donald Glover's name in an Instagram Story captioned "Dont worry we have more for yall," indicating at least another collaboration between the two. It is likely that Yeat recorded his vocals for the track during the same session as "Power Trip."

Notes:
(April 10, 2024) (Yeat announces LYFESTYLE and A DANGEROUS LYFE)
(January 2025) (Yeat silently shifts his plans to A Dangerous Lyfe/Love contrast behind the scenes)
Leak Date: On April 10, 2024, Yeat announced in an Instagram Story that he was going to drop 2 more albums this year to "show u who do what," LYFESTYLE and A DANGEROUS LYFE. On August 20, 2024, several screenshots from an Instagram groupchat between Yeat and fanpages leaked, one in which where he stated that "ADL song[s] [are] complicated" and that they "Take like 25-30 [minutes]," compared to how LYFESTYLE tracks "take like 5-10 [minutes]." This period featured a wide range of experimental sounds, as he messed around with using newer sounds as well as returning to some of his old style. Though originally intended for release by the end of 2024, Yeat delayed the album.

Notes:

(May 24, 2025) (Yeat begins to tease DANGEROUS SUMMER)
Leak Date: After pushing back the original 2024 release date for A DANGEROUS LYFE, Yeat had a change in plans for the album. Following a breakup from his 3-year-relationship, Yeat began to play with the idea of "love" in addition to his "Lyfe" theme. He gets "ADL" tatted on his arm, which in an interview with InkedMag he reveals stands for both "A Dangerous Lyfe" and "A Dangerous Love," which silently teased the potential for a double album. The cover art for this era is an ADL logo shared by Yeat in an Instagram Story on April 3, 2025, which he also briefly made his profile picture.

Teased by Yeat on [untitled] in an Instagram story captioned "WEN I DROP DIS ALBUM IT WONT EVER Bë THE SAM3…" on April 19, 2025. He also mentioned the white ADL cover with the white text is "NOT THE COVER…"

Following the announcement on March 27, 2025 of Don Toliver and Yeat headlining Summer Smash Festival on Friday, June 20, Pitchfork released an article in which they mentioned that "the festival will feature Don Toliver and Yeat (debuting a new collaborative project)." However, the next day, a Summer Smash PR team member later denied this claim, suggesting that their performance is "a one-time, exclusive joint set - not a teaser for new collaborative music," thus leading to Pitchfork updating their article. They ended up performing two unreleased tracks during their set, however, it is possible they had recorded more.

On April 6, 2025, Justin Bieber posted an Instagram post which Yeat liked and commented "💿💿💿💿💿💿💿💿" on. Same day, Justin Bieber posted an Instagram post captioned "Dm me if u wanna make music together." Two days later on April 8, 2025, Justin Bieber reposted Yeat's Instagram post previewing "the bell" to his Story with the caption "YEATCHELLA," which Yeat reposted to his own Story captioned "💿💿brudda🚜💨," sparking rumors of a collaboration, though it may be at most a co-sign.

In Yeat's Grailed Limited Zine cover story drop, the author writes: "Even though he's stopped speaking, Yeat's voice blares throughout the studio, as new, unreleased music plays, including more than 10 unheard collaborations with Lil Uzi Vert, all made in the past week." This likely includes "how you doing" alongside others; it is unconfirmed when the "past week" is referring to, however, it is most likely prior to May 23, 2025.
On Discord, Lunchbox mentioned that "Yeat asked [him] to be on ADL but  [he] ain't hear from em in a min so we'll see."
Yeat posted a picture of himself and Yung Kayo in an Instagram Story captioned "DANGERUZZLYFE" on April 25, 2025, teasing a possible collaboration.

Julia revealed in an interview on the Zach Sang Show on October 27, 2025, that Drake had originally asked her to send him some of her demos, so she began to record over Scro loops. She came up with what ended up becoming "DOG HOUSE," which was originally a longer song, which Drake ended up taking the ending chorus to use for the final track. Drake resonated with it, and Julia did not end up hearing it again until much later. Leak Date: ADL, which stands for A Dangerous Lyfe and A Dangerous Love, is Yeat's sixth studio album. It is his longest worked on project, having been conceptualized for nearly two years. Following the release of DANGEROUS SUMMER, Yeat's full focus is back to ADL. During this period, Yeat worked extensively, collaborating with new artists and working in different studios, including Rick Rubin's Shangri-La. In March 2026, Yeat finally announced the release date of the album and officially revealed the project was going to be a double album, not solely A Dangerous Lyfe, but also A Dangerous Love, which Yeat had left hints of throughout the process, dating back over a year in advance.
